rulings.Case Title: ROBERT RUHL et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 28, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant FDCC California Inc.¿s motion to quash service of plaintiff¿s summons is granted. Plaintiff¿s summons fails to specify that FDCC was being served as a Doe. CCP § 474 provides that a summons must provide a DOE defendant with notice of the fictitious name under which it was designated in the complaint. In Armstrong v. Superior Court (1956) 144 Cal.App.2d 420, 424 the court held that when a summons fails to identify the fictitious name under which a defendant is being sued, the ¿effect is to make the service of such a defective summons insufficient to give the court jurisdiction over the person of the defendant.¿ (See also Carol Gilbert, Inc. v. Haller 179 Cal.Rpt.3d 843). =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD PALMER VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 28, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Pneumo-Abex¿s motion for summary adjudication as to punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production since plaintiff¿s interrogatory responses were not factually devoid. Further, Dr. Castelman¿s declaration and Pneumo-Abex¿ s interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether Pneumo-Abex knew its asbestos-containing products were harmful at a time before plaintiff claims exposure to them yet defendant continued to market them. Pneumo-Abex¿s objections to Dr Castelman¿s declaration are overruled. Dr. Castelman has sufficient foundation and expertise to base his statements that Abex knew of the dangers of asbestos. =(220/HEK)
